For writes: lists groups or shards that missed per-group quorum (§2 write path). For reads: lists shards that could not be covered. Absence of the header means full coverage. | §2 write path, §3 `unavailable_shard_policy` |\n1004-| `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` | Response | Monotonically increasing version of the committed index settings. Stamped on responses after a successful two-phase settings commit. Clients may echo this value back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` on subsequent reads for session-consistent behavior. | §13.5 |\n1005-| `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` | Request | Client-supplied floor (`u64`) for settings freshness. Request header; the orchestrator excludes nodes with `node_settings_version` below this floor when building a covering set (§13.5), and returns HTTP 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` only if no covering set can be assembled after exclusions. Intended to be populated from a previously observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version`. | §13.5 |\n1006:| `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` | Response | Warning: the response was served while a two-phase settings broadcast was mid-flight (propose / verify window). Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n1007-| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n1008-| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n1009-| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n1010-| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n1011-| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n--\n2404-    if diverge:   reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair);\n2405-                  after max_retries, freeze writes on that index and\n2406-                  raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.\n2407-```\n2408-\n2409:During phases 1–2 the orchestrator returns 202 with a \"pending\" status; clients poll `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}`. Reads during that window include an `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.\n2410-\n2411-**Drift reconciler (always on).** A background task runs every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5m), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. This catches out-of-band changes (direct access to a node) and cures drift without operator intervention.\n2412-\n2413-[Omitted long context line]\n2414-"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3407e25c-a0e3-4541-82b9-cbb28842d528","uuid":"6fdd51b0-d878-4bd3-8687-d60896210ded","timestamp":"2026-05-23T11:34:28.649Z","tool_use_result":{"mode":"content","numFiles":0,"filenames":[],"content":"1001-| Header | Direction | Semantics | Defined in |\n1002-|--------|-----------|-----------|------------|\n1003-| `X-Miroir-Degraded` | Response | Write or read completed with reduced redundancy. Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n1007-| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n1008-| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n1009-| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n1010-| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n1011-| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n--\n2404-    if diverge:   reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair);\n2405-                  after max_retries, freeze writes on that index and\n2406-                  raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.\n2407-```\n2408-\n2409:During phases 1–2 the orchestrator returns 202 with a \"pending\" status; clients poll `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}`. Reads during that window include an `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.\n2410-\n2411-**Drift reconciler (always on).** A background task runs every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5m), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. This catches out-of-band changes (direct access to a node) and cures drift without operator intervention.\n2412-\n2413-[Omitted long context line]\n2414-","numLines":23}}

Parallel PATCH with Task Succession\n41\t\n42\t### Architecture\n43\t\n44\t```\n45\tPhase 1: Propose (parallel)\n46\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n47\t│ 1. Poll each node's task until all reach \"succeeded\"        │\n54\t│    └─ Parallel polling with exponential backoff             │\n55\t│    └─ Use existing NodePoller trait from task_registry.rs   │\n56\t│    └─ Timeout: verify_timeout_s from config                 │\n57\t│                                                              │\n58\t│ 4. On all succeeded → enter Phase 2                          │\n59\t│    On any failed/timeout → abort broadcast                   │\n60\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n61\t\n62\tDuring Phase 1: Response header X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent: true\n63\t```\n64\t\n65\t### Implementation Details\n66\t\n67\t#### Step 1: Parallel PATCH (using existing pattern from scatter.rs)\n68\t\n69\t```rust\n70\t// Location: crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/indexes.rs\n71\t// After line 838 in two_phase_settings_broadcast()\n72\t\n73\tuse futures_util::future::join_all;\n74\t\n75\t// Parallel PATCH to all nodes\n76\tlet patch_tasks: Vec<_> = nodes.iter().map(|address| {\n77\t    let client = client.clone();\n78\t    let address = address.clone();\n79\t    let full_path = full_path.clone();\n80\t    let body = body.clone();\n81\t    async move {\n82\t        (address.clone(), client.patch_raw(&address, &full_path, &body).await)\n83\t    }\n84\t}).collect();\n85\t\n86\tlet patch_results = join_all(patch_tasks).await;\n87\t\n88\t// Collect task_uids and first response\n89\tlet mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\n90\tlet mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n91\tlet mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n92\t\n93\tfor (address, result) in patch_results {\n94\t    match result {\n95\t        Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n96\t            if first_response.is_none() {\n97\t                first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n98\t            }\n99\t            if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n100\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n101\t                    node_task_uids.insert(address, task_uid);\n102\t                }\n103\t            }\n104\t        }\n105\t        Ok((status, text)) => {\n106\t            errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n107\t        }\n108\t        Err(e) => {\n109\t            errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n110\t        }\n111\t    }\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\tif !errors.is_empty() {\n115\t    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n116\t        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n117\t        format!(\"Phase 1 propose failed: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n118\t    ));\n119\t}\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\t#### Step 2: Task Succession (polling until all succeeded)\n123\t\n124\t```rust\n125\t// New helper function: poll_all_tasks_until_succeeded()\n126\t// Location: crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/indexes.rs\n127\t\n128\tuse crate::scatter::TaskStatusRequest;\n129\tuse std::time::Duration;\n130\t\n131\t/// Poll all node tasks until they reach terminal state.\n132\t/// Returns Ok(()) if all succeeded, Err on any failure or timeout.\n133\tasync fn poll_all_tasks_until_succeeded(\n134\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n135\t    nodes: &[String],\n136\t    node_task_uids: &HashMap<String, u64>,\n137\t    timeout_s: u64,\n138\t) -> Result<(), MeilisearchError> {\n139\t    let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n140\t    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(timeout_s);\n141\t    let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n142\t    let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n143\t\n144\t    loop {\n145\t        // Check timeout\n146\t        if start.elapsed() > timeout {\n147\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n148\t                MiroirCode::Timeout,\n149\t                format!(\"Phase 1 task succession timed out after {}s\", timeout_s),\n150\t            ));\n151\t        }\n152\t\n153\t        // Parallel task status polling\n154\t        let poll_tasks: Vec<_> = node_task_uids.iter().map(|(address, &task_uid)| {\n155\t            let client = client.clone();\n156\t            let address = address.clone();\n157\t            async move {\n158\t                let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid };\n159\t                (address.clone(), client.get_task_status_raw(&address, &req).await)\n160\t            }\n161\t        }).collect();\n162\t\n163\t        let poll_results = join_all(poll_tasks).await;\n164\t\n165\t        // Track terminal states\n166\t        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n167\t        let mut any_failed = false;\n168\t        let mut all_terminal = true;\n169\t\n170\t        for (address, result) in poll_results {\n171\t            match result {\n172\t                Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n173\t                    if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n174\t                        if let Some(task_status) = resp.get(\"status\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n175\t                            match task_status {\n176\t                                \"succeeded\" => {}\n177\t                                \"failed\" => {\n178\t                                    any_failed = true;\n179\t                                    all_terminal = true;\n180\t                                }\n181\t                                _ => {\n182\t }\n187\t                    }\n188\t                }\n189\t                Ok(_) | Err(_) => {\n190\t                    // Treat poll errors as non-terminal (retry)\n191\t                    all_terminal = false;\n192\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n193\t                }\n194\t            }\n195\t        }\n196\t\n197\t        // Check completion conditions\n198\t        if reaches \"failed\" status | Abort immediately, return error |\n272\t| Task succession timeout | Abort with Timeout error |\n273\t| Leadership loss during Phase 1 | Persist state via ModeBOpLeader, new leader resumes |\n274\t\n275\t### Integration with Existing Components\n276\t\n277\t1. **SettingsBroadcast**: Already has `start_propose()` and `enter_verify()` methods\n278\t2. **NodePoller trait**: P5.5.a Proposal: Phase 1 — Parallel PATCH with Task Succession\n\n**Bead:** miroir-uhj.5.1\n**Date:** 2026-05-23\n**Plan Reference:** §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast with verification\n\n## Current State Analysis\n\n### Existing Implementation Location\n`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/indexes.rs` — `two_phase_settings_broadcast()` function (lines 809-1039)\n\n### Current Phase 1 Flow (Sequential)\n\n```rust\n// Lines 839-868: Current implementation\nfor address in &nodes {\n    match client.patch_raw(address, &full_path, body).await {\n        Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n            // Extract taskUid if present\n            if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(address.clone(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        // ... error handling\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Findings:**\n\n1. **Sequential execution**: The `for` loop processes nodes one-by-one, not in parallel\n2. **No task awaiting**: The code collects `task_uid`s but does NOT poll/wait for tasks to reach \"succeeded\"\n3. **Misleading comment**: Line 887 says \"Wait for all node tasks to complete\" but the `run_verify` closure immediately does GET requests instead of polling task status\n4. **Verification bypasses task status**: Phase 2 uses GET /indexes/{uid}/settings to verify hashes, not task status polling\n\n**Problem**: This violates plan §13.5 which explicitly states \"await all task_uids to reach succeeded\" as Phase 1's completion condition.\n\n## Proposed Phase 1: Parallel PATCH with Task Succession\n\n### Architecture\n\n```\nPhase 1: Propose (parallel)\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ 1. Spawn parallel PATCH requests to all nodes                │\n│    └─ Use futures_util::future::join_all (existing pattern) │\n│                                                              │\n│ 2. Collect task_uid from each PATCH response                │\n│    └─ Store in HashMap<String, u64> (node_id -> task_uid)    │\n│                                                              │\n│ 3. Poll each node's task until all reach \"succeeded\"        │\n│    └─ Parallel polling with exponential backoff             │\n│    └─ Use existing NodePoller trait from task_registry.rs   │\n│    └─ Timeout: verify_timeout_s from config                 │\n│                                                              │\n│ 4. On all succeeded → enter Phase 2                          │\n│    On any failed/timeout → abort broadcast                   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n\nDuring Phase 1: Response header X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent: true\n```\n\n### Implementation Details\n\n#### Step 1: Parallel PATCH (using existing pattern from scatter.rs)\n\n```rust\n// Location: crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/indexes.rs\n// After line 838 in two_phase_settings_broadcast()\n\nuse futures_util::future::join_all;\n\n// Parallel PATCH to all nodes\nlet patch_tasks: Vec<_> = nodes.iter().map(|address| {\n    let client = client.clone();\n    let address = address.clone();\n    let full_path = full_path.clone();\n    let body = body.clone();\n    async move {\n        (address.clone(), client.patch_raw(&address, &full_path, &body).await)\n    }\n}).collect();\n\nlet patch_results = join_all(patch_tasks).await;\n\n// Collect task_uids and first response\nlet mut node_task_uids = HashMap::new();\nlet mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\nlet mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\nfor (address, result) in patch_results {\n    match result {\n        Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n            if first_response.is_none() {\n                first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n            }\n            if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(address, task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        Ok((status, text)) => {\n            errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nif !errors.is_empty() {\n    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n        format!(\"Phase 1 propose failed: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n    ));\n}\n```\n\n#### Step 2: Task Succession (polling until all succeeded)\n\n```rust\n// New helper function: poll_all_tasks_until_succeeded()\n// Location: crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/indexes.rs\n\nuse crate::scatter::TaskStatusRequest;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// Poll all node tasks until they reach terminal state.\n/// Returns Ok(()) if all succeeded, Err on any failure or timeout.\nasync fn poll_all_tasks_until_succeeded(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    nodes: &[String],\n    node_task_uids: &HashMap<String, u64>,\n    timeout_s: u64,\n) -> Result<(), MeilisearchError> {\n    let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(timeout_s);\n    let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n    let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n    loop {\n        // Check timeout\n        if start.elapsed() > timeout {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::Timeout,\n                format!(\"Phase 1 task succession timed out after {}s\", timeout_s),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Parallel task status polling\n        let poll_tasks: Vec<_> = node_task_uids.iter().map(|(address, &task_uid)| {\n            let client = client.clone();\n            let address = address.clone();\n            async move {\n                let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid };\n                (address.clone(), client.get_task_status_raw(&address, &req).await)\n            }\n        }).collect();\n\n        let poll_results = join_all(poll_tasks).await;\n\n        // Track terminal states\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n        let mut all_terminal = true;\n\n        for (address, result) in poll_results {\n            match result {\n                Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                    if let Ok(resp) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&text) {\n                        if let Some(task_status) = resp.get(\"status\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n                            match task_status {\n                                \"succeeded\" => {}\n                                \"failed\" => {\n                                    any_failed = true;\n                                    all_terminal = true;\n                                }\n                                _ => {\n                                    all_terminal = false;\n                                    all_succeeded = false;\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(_) | Err(_) => {\n                    // Treat poll errors as non-terminal (retry)\n                    all_terminal = false;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check completion conditions\n        if any_failed {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                \"Phase 1: at least one node task failed\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Exponential backoff\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n        delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n    }\n}\n\n// Call it after collecting task_uids:\npoll_all_tasks_until_succeeded(&client, &nodes, &node_task_uids, config.settings_broadcast.verify_timeout_s).await?;\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Add MeilisearchClient::get_task_status_raw()\n\n```rust\n// Location: crates/miroir-proxy/src/client.rs (or wherever MeilisearchClient is defined)\n\nimpl MeilisearchClient {\n    /// Get task status from a node (raw response).\n    pub async fn get_task_status_raw(\n        &self,\n        address: &str,\n        req: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> Result<(u16, String), reqwest::Error> {\n        let path = format!(\"/tasks/{}\", req.task_uid);\n        self.get_raw(address, &path).await\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Performance Characteristics\n\n| Metric | Sequential (Current) | Parallel (Proposed) |\n|--------|---------------------|---------------------|\n| PATCH latency | O(N × t_patch) | O(max(t_patch)) |\n| Task poll rounds | O(N × rounds) | O(rounds) |\n| Total Phase 1 time | ~N × (50ms + 500ms) | ~max(50ms) + rounds × 25ms |\n| 10 nodes @ 50ms PATCH | ~550ms | ~50ms + poll time |\n\n**Example**: For 10 nodes with 50ms PATCH latency:\n- Sequential: 10 × 50ms = 500ms just for PATCH requests\n- Parallel: max(50ms) = 50ms for all PATCH requests\n- **9x speedup** on the PATCH phase alone\n\n### X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent Header\n\nDuring Phase 1, search responses should include the warning header:\n\n```rust\n// Location: crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs\n// In search handler, after scattering results:\n\nif state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(index).await {\n    headers.insert(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\".parse().unwrap());\n}\n```\n\nThis signals to clients that ranking scores may be inconsistent across nodes during the broadcast.\n\n### Error Handling\n\n| Scenario | Action |\n|----------|--------|\n| Any PATCH fails (HTTP 4xx/5xx) | Abort immediately, return error |\n| Task reaches \"failed\" status | Abort immediately, return error |\n| Task succession timeout | Abort with Timeout error |\n| Leadership loss during Phase 1 | Persist state via ModeBOpLeader, new leader resumes |\n\n### Integration with Existing Components\n\n1. **SettingsBroadcast**: Already has `start_propose()` and `enter_verify()` methods\n2. **NodePoller trait**: Exists in `task_registry.rs` for polling abstraction\n3. **Parallel execution pattern**: `join_all` already used in `scatter.rs`\n4. **MeilisearchClient**: Has `patch_raw()` method, needs `get_task_status_raw()`\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n### Unit Tests\n1. **Parallel PATCH success**: Mock 10 nodes, verify all PATCH requests issued concurrently\n2. **Task succession happy path**: Mock task status progression enqueued → processing → succeeded\n3. **Task failure handling**: Mock one task reaching \"failed\", verify abort\n4. **Timeout handling**: Mock tasks never completing, verify timeout error\n\n### Integration Tests\n```rust\n// parallel\n    // Verify Phase 2 entered after all succeeded\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_parallel_propose_one_fails() {\n    // 3-node cluster, node-1 returns 500\n    // Verify immediate abort, no Phase 2\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_settings_inconsistent_header() {\n    // Start Phase 1\n    // Issue search request\n    // Verify X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent: true\n}\n```\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. **Should we use the existing InMemoryTaskRegistry polling or inline polling?**\n   - Inline polling is simpler for this one-shot use case\n   - TaskRegistry polling is better for long-lived task tracking\n\n2. **Should Phase 1 persist task_uids to task_store before polling?**\n   - Yes: allows new leader to resume after leadership loss\n   - Use `SettingsBroadcastCoordinator.enter_verify()` which persists\n\n3. **What about PATCH requests that return without a taskUid?**\n   - Should not happen in normal Meilisearch operation\n   - Treat as
